title: The 2023 Oscar Nominations: Snubs, Surprises, and WTFs
format: Sean and Amanda go through the 2023 Oscar nominations morning-of, then Sean sits down with Lukas Dhont about Close.

segments.heading: Best Picture: A Balanced Ballot
segments.note: the ten nominees, category by category

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: the blockbuster they'd been begging the Academy to nominate.

films.title: Elvis
films.note: the traditional biopic slot.

films.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
films.note: leads all nominations with 11; the Best Picture front-runner all season.

films.title: Tár
films.note: Todd Field's third film, third Best Picture nod.

films.title: Triangle of Sadness
films.note: Amanda called this one early.

films.title: All Quiet on the Western Front
films.note: came out of nowhere in the last two months to rack up nine nominations.

films.title: The Fabelmans
films.note: Spielberg's late-period Old Hollywood entry.

films.title: The Whale
films.note: one of the two films they predicted for Best Picture that missed.

films.title: Babylon
films.note: Amanda's wish-casting pick; it missed too.

segments.heading: Does the Nomination Leader Ever Win?
segments.note: Amanda's year-by-year rundown of the film with the most nominations

films.title: The Power of the Dog
films.note: 12 nominations, lost Best Picture to Coda.

films.title: CODA
films.note: won Best Picture with just 3 nominations total.

films.title: Belfast
films.note: 7 nominations.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: also 7 nominations.

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: skipped in the rundown as "the COVID year."

films.title: Joker
films.note: led the year with 11 nominations.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: tied for second with 10, won nothing.
also 10 nominations.

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: also 10 nominations.

films.title: Parasite
films.note: won Best Picture with only 6 nominations.

films.title: The Favourite
films.note: tied the year's lead with 10 nominations.

films.title: Roma
films.note: also 10 nominations.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: 8 nominations.

films.title: Vice
films.note: also 8 nominations.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: 7 nominations.

films.title: BlacKkKlansman
films.note: 6 nominations.

films.title: Green Book
films.note: won Best Picture with just 5 nominations.

films.title: The Shape of Water
films.note: led with 14 nominations, and won.

films.title: La La Land
films.note: led with 14 nominations, lost Best Picture.

films.title: Moonlight
films.note: won with 8 nominations.

films.title: Arrival
films.note: tied Moonlight with 8 nominations.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: named again later as one of that year's favorites.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: named again later as one of that year's favorites.

segments.heading: The Andrea Riseborough Campaign
segments.note: how a grassroots push landed a Best Actress nomination

films.title: To Leslie
films.note: barely released, but a two-week Twitter and screening campaign got Andrea Riseborough into Best Actress over higher-profile contenders.

segments.heading: Best Actress and Supporting Actor Snubs

films.title: The Woman King
films.note: Viola Davis left out of Best Actress; the hosts call it a missed campaign, not a bad movie.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: the Ana de Armas performance they'd been championing for years.

films.title: Blonde
films.note: her actual Best Actress-nominated performance.

films.title: Causeway
films.note: Brian Tyree Henry's Best Supporting Actor nomination, for a quiet, small-scale film.

films.title: Eileen
films.note: Sean watched it the night before; Ottessa Moshfegh's second script with husband Luke Goebel.

segments.heading: The Menu and the Cinematography Category

films.title: The Menu
films.note: Amanda argues Hong Chau's Best Supporting Actress nomination is really for this, even though it's credited to The Whale.

films.title: Empire of Light
films.note: Roger Deakins' surprising Best Cinematography nod for a film they otherwise didn't care for.

films.title: Bardo, False Chronicle of a Handful of Truths
films.note: Darius Khondji's cinematography nomination for a movie that missed Best International Feature entirely.

films.title: M3GAN
films.note: in passing, as a box-office comparison for Puss in Boots.

segments.heading: Animation, Song, and a Few Curiosities

films.title: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
Puss in Boots: The Last Wish
films.note: still packing kids into theaters in its fifth week.

films.title: The Sea Beast
films.note: the Netflix animated nominee, loosely a Moby-Dick riff.

films.title: RRR
films.note: "Naatu Naatu" landed a Best Song nomination; the film itself was shut out everywhere else.

films.title: Tell It Like a Woman
films.note: Diane Warren's 14th Oscar nomination, for the song "Applause," from a film neither host had heard of.

films.title: Skinamarink
films.note: Sean's been singing its lullaby to his son.

films.title: Nope
films.note: shut out completely; they chalk it up to a slow-building reappraisal that came too late in the year.

films.title: A House Made of Splinters
films.note: Best Documentary nominee neither host has seen.

films.title: Good Night Oppy
films.note: not even shortlisted, seen as too crowd-pleasing for the doc branch.

films.title: All the Beauty and the Bloodshed
Descendant
films.note: Margaret Brown's Netflix film, snubbed.

films.title: Moonage Daydream
films.note: Brett Morgen's film, snubbed.

films.title: Retrograde
films.note: Matthew Heineman's film, snubbed despite Stanley Tucci's public advocacy.

segments.heading: Best International Feature and Closing Thoughts

films.title: The Quiet Girl
films.note: Ireland's big morning; a film Amanda hasn't seen yet.

films.title: Close
films.note: Belgian director Lukas Dhont's Best International Feature nominee, opening this week; sets up the interview.

films.title: Black Panther: Wakanda Forever
films.note: five nominations, barely discussed on the show so far.

films.title: Aftersun
films.note: a rewatch the hosts keep promising each other.

segments.heading: In Conversation With Lukas Dhont
segments.note: on Close, ahead of its Best International Feature nomination

films.title: Close
films.note: his second feature; about the intense, wordless friendship between two 13-year-old boys.

films.title: Girl
films.note: his 2018 debut.

films.title: Jeanne Dielman, 23, quai du Commerce, 1080 Bruxelles
films.note: Chantal Akerman's film, an influence on him in film school, later named the greatest film ever made by Sight and Sound.

films.title: Kid
films.note: by fellow Flemish filmmaker Fien Troch, which he recommends.

films.title: Home
films.note: another Fien Troch film he recommends.

films.title: The Eight Mountains
films.note: one of several 2022 films he sees as part of a wave about male friendship.

films.title: Saint Omer
films.note: his answer to "the last great thing you've seen," France's international submission.

films.title: Aftersun
films.note: his other pick, for how it handles mental health.
